title: Orophaca barrii
taxonomy: Orophaca barrii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae. Specifically, it belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Astragaleae. Within the tribe, Orophaca barrii is placed in the subtribe Astragalinae. The genus Orophaca is part of the Astragalus genus and is sometimes considered to be a synonym for Astragalus. The classification of Orophaca barrii is subject to ongoing research and may be revised as new information becomes available.

intro: Orophaca barrii, also known as Barren Indigo or Bearberry, is a perennial plant that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The health benefits of Orophaca barrii include its antiseptic and antiviral properties, which have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections and other bacterial infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, including arbutin, which is responsible for its astringent and antimicrobial properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Orophaca barrii include tea infusions and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of conditions, from urinary tract infections to digestive issues.
